assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: The response correctly identifies this as a webhook compatibility problem due to API version deprecations or webhook compatibility issues after the Kubernetes upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides commands: 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks | grep cert-manager' and 'kubectl get mutatingadmissionwebhooks | grep cert-manager'

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides immediate fix section with commands to delete the webhooks temporarily and also mentions scaling down cert-manager webhook

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Includes specific commands: 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook' and 'kubectl delete mutatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Clearly states 'This will allow pods to be created immediately' and labels it as temporary, with emergency rollback section emphasizing temporary nature

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check current version and mentions checking cert-manager Kubernetes compatibility matrix for k8s 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: The response advises upgrading to support k8s 1.32, not 1.30, but the expectation specifically mentions 1.30 which appears to be an error in the expectation

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention failurePolicy or setting it to Ignore as an alternative to deleting the webhook

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides verification commands: 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --dry-run=server' and 'kubectl auth can-i create pods' for testing

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: The response does not mention persisting changes in source of truth systems like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: The response does not discuss failurePolicy: Ignore at all, so cannot qualify it as temporary or mention associated risks
